req.title: Manager creates leave-request
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a leave-request through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates UI-based leave-request creation by an authenticated manager. Test design covers: (1) EP positive path – all valid mandatory fields submitted by a manager yields 200/201 plus audit record; (2) NEG path – missing mandatory field yields 400 validation error; (3) BVA boundary – leave duration of exactly 1 day (minimum boundary); (4) NEG+STC-aligned negative – unauthenticated actor is rejected with 401; the heuristic is corrected to NEG since the test exercises a negative/error pathway rather than a pure state-transition sequence. The 401 scenario is driven by the error_pathways in the requirement and is best classified as NEG. (5) DT decision-table – role=employee (non-manager) is rejected with 403. This gives ≥1 positive, ≥1 negative, ≥1 boundary case and covers all cited error pathways. The fix for the reported violation is: test[3] heuristic changed from STC to NEG because the test is kind=negative and STC is reserved for state-transition coverage tests.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_leave_request_all_valid_fields
preconditions: Valid payload with all mandatory fields is available
actions: POST /leave-requests with valid JSON payload as authenticated manager
Capture HTTP response status and body
Query audit log for the returned entity id
expected: Response body contains a non-null entity id
Audit record exists for the created leave-request id
trace: R-WEB-013 – audit record written on success
R-WEB-013 – response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_creates_leave_request_all_valid_fields(
    manager_session,       # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session for manager
    base_url,              # <fixture> returns app base URL string
    valid_leave_payload,   # <fixture> returns dict with all mandatory fields
    audit_log_client,      # <fixture> returns helper to query audit records
    cleanup_leave_request  # <fixture> deletes created resource after test
):
    resp = manager_session.post(
        f"{base_url}/leave-requests",
        json=valid_leave_payload
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200 or 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id")
    assert entity_id, "Response body must contain a non-null entity id"

    cleanup_leave_request(entity_id)

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.find_by_entity_id(entity_id)
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, (
        f"No audit record found for leave-request id={entity_id}"
    )

tests.name: test_create_leave_request_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: Payload is prepared with one mandatory field omitted
actions: POST /leave-requests with payload missing the 'start_date' mandatory field
Capture HTTP response status and error body
expected: Response body contains validation error referencing the missing field

tests.name: test_create_leave_request_single_day_boundary_duration
preconditions: Payload uses start_date == end_date (duration = 1 day, minimum boundary)
actions: POST /leave-requests with start_date equal to end_date
Capture HTTP response status and body
Verify entity id is present and audit record is written
expected: Response body contains a non-null entity id
Audit record exists confirming the 1-day leave was persisted
trace: R-WEB-013 – boundary: minimum valid leave duration = 1 day
R-WEB-013 – audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date

def test_create_leave_request_single_day_boundary_duration(
    manager_session,       # <fixture>
    base_url,              # <fixture>
    valid_leave_payload,   # <fixture>
    audit_log_client,      # <fixture>
    cleanup_leave_request  # <fixture>
):
    today = date.today().isoformat()
    boundary_payload = {**valid_leave_payload, "start_date": today, "end_date": today}

    resp = manager_session.post(
        f"{base_url}/leave-requests",
        json=boundary_payload
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200/201 for 1-day leave, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    entity_id = resp.json().get("id")
    assert entity_id, "Entity id must be present in response"

    cleanup_leave_request(entity_id)

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.find_by_entity_id(entity_id)
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, (
        f"No audit record for 1-day leave-request id={entity_id}"
    )

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401
expected: System remains in unauthenticated state; no leave-request is created
trace: NEG: invalid actor class (unauthenticated) is rejected before reaching creation state

tests.name: test_employee_role_forbidden_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated as role:employee (not manager)
Valid payload with all mandatory fields is available
actions: POST /leave-requests with valid payload using an employee-role session
trace: DT: decision column – authenticated=true, role=employee → deny
